findings.message: loadItemsQuietly() catches the fetchItems() rejection and returns [] with no rethrow and no telemetry. Because the rejection is caught here, the promise never rejects, so the global 'unhandledrejection' handler in observability/rum.ts (the only path into the RUM js-errors counter) never fires. The component also no longer calls setError(true), so the load failure is invisible to both the user and RUM — a failed API path renders as an empty 'No items yet.' state with zero observability.
findings.suggestion: Do not swallow the error. Either remove loadItemsQuietly and restore the .catch(() => setError(true)) handler so the failure is surfaced to the user, and/or emit telemetry (e.g. rethrow so 'unhandledrejection' fires, or push an {kind:'error'} RUM event / increment the js-errors counter) before returning the empty fallback so the failure reaches the RUM error signal.
